items.rationale: Seen as crypto's clearest product-market fit and the first realistic path to onboard a billion people; volumes at all-time highs (~$33T/yr, rivaling Visa), uncorrelated with trading = organic use. GENIUS Act (signed July 2025) gave legal clarity.
items.age_note: Called 'killer app' since 2024-09; conviction intensified through 2025 with legislation and volume ATHs

citations.quote: Stablecoins now present what I believe is the first credible opportunity to onboard a billion people into crypto.
Stablecoins have found product market fit and this chart shows it.
Stablecoins are the "killer app" of crypto, and here's a bunch of great data that shows it.

items.rationale: AI agents need crypto rails to transact at machine speed; crypto provides trustworthy human-feedback data, IP provenance, identity/bot-resistance, and guardrails. Multiple recent investments (Catena, Yupp, Poseidon, Ambient).
items.age_note: Emerged mid-2024 ('AI agents can have crypto wallets'), became a dominant 2025 thesis

citations.quote: Machine-speed systems need machine-speed money. Catena is building the missing link
This time is no different: AI needs blockchain-enabled computing.
AI needs strong, reliable evaluation based on large-scale human input. Crypto is the trust machine that can help deliver it.

items.rationale: Framed as the native antidote to physical-infrastructure monopolies; user-generated services for the physical world. Strict definition (must touch physical world, not cloud/mining). Daylight, distributed energy a focus.
items.age_note: Consistent enthusiasm 2024–2025; 'eating the world' framing by mid-2025

citations.quote: DePIN is eating the world
DePIN provides the most salient path towards making atoms look more like bits for builders and investors alike.

items.rationale: Privacy now seen as the key bottleneck for institutional and consumer adoption; 'radical privacy by default.' Heavy recent deal flow (Seismic, Miden, Aztec, Aleo, RAILGUN) and apps hitting ATHs.
items.age_note: Built through 2024 (Aleo, coSNARKs); became explicit priority bottleneck by 2025

citations.quote: We have the technology to make radical privacy the default. Let's build it.
People require privacy for most of their financial activity.

items.rationale: Core house thesis: tokens should derive real value from decentralized networks (protocol fees, burns) — not be pie-chart governance tokens. New regulatory clarity (CLARITY) finally enables sustainable token economies.
items.age_note: Value-accrual theme constant; 'era is here' declared 2025-08 as regulation unlocked it

citations.quote: The era of network tokens with decentralized onchain revenue is here. The era of crypto offshoring is over.
Network tokens should have real economic value. It's the only way forward.

items.rationale: Growing steadily (DEX/CEX ratio up to 17%); seen as benefiting most from stablecoin + market-structure legislation. Strong view that market structure 'can't work without protecting DeFi.'

citations.quote: Market structure can't work without protecting DeFi.
DeFi stands to benefit from that shift

items.rationale: Viewed as under-hyped, foundational scaling/verification primitive that democratizes ZK via familiar instruction sets (RISC-V) and lookups; proving networks deemed 'essential.' Tempered by sober security/performance caveats (see concerns).
items.age_note: Bullish since Jolt launch 2024-04; 2025-03 added strong 'not ready for prime time' caution on bugs/perf

citations.quote: I still think ZK is under hyped.
I believe proving networks will become essential for blockchains

items.rationale: Named as a category 'obviously cooking'; bringing RWAs onchain to reduce friction and expand markets. Pushing safe harbors so tokenized securities can trade onchain.

citations.quote: Bringing real world assets onchain to reduce friction and expand markets (RWA tokenization)
This is critical for tokenized securities to trade onchain.

items.rationale: NFTs enable digital property rights, creator-fan direct relationships, and an IP coordination layer for the AI-content explosion (Story Protocol $80M). Pushing for collectibles safe harbor grounded in consumptive demand.

citations.quote: We desperately need an open, global coordination layer for IP, and I couldn't be more excited about what Story is building!
The evolution of NFTs from PFPs to true digital scarcity across games, media, and collectibles

items.rationale: Open protocols (Farcaster) enable interoperable, user-owned identity/data and counter Big Tech consolidation; viewed as a competition antidote.
items.age_note: Steady interest 2024; fewer fresh restatements by mid-2025

citations.quote: The Decentralized Social experiment is underway, and vibrant developer ecosystems are emerging around open protocols like Farcaster.

items.rationale: Seen as zero-value casino activity that deters serious founders, arms hostile regulators, and damages adoption — distinct from network tokens. Legal but valueless; not a substitute for productive use cases.
items.age_note: Strong stance since 2024-04; by 2025 SEC's reasonable memecoin posture eased legal risk but disdain for the activity persisted

citations.quote: we shouldn't pretend that the casino doesn't set us back. Deterring great founders and giving ammo to politicized regulators has a profound effect.
True memecoins have no intrinsic value (no utility, no company, no anything) and their price is driven purely by speculation

items.rationale: Now-unnecessary post-legislation; create operational drag, conflicts, tax risk, and obfuscate real control. Used to shortcut decentralization rather than achieve it. Push to replace with PBCs + DUNA/BORG tooling.
items.age_note: Crescendo in 2025; 'time to move on' declared 2025-06 as regulatory shift removed their rationale

citations.quote: It's time for crypto move on from the "foundation" model.
some consultants are manufacturing a sense of urgency to push them into foreign foundations as early as possible

items.rationale: Senate 'ancillary asset' draft punts hard questions to regulators, opens loopholes, and weakens transfer-restriction framework — re-creating incentives to dodge decentralization and dump on retail. Tornado Cash ruling shows risk of unchecked agency discretion.
items.age_note: Fresh concern (2025-07/08) layered onto otherwise-improving regulatory optimism

citations.quote: The undermining of CLARITY's transfer restriction framework creates short-term incentives to circumvent decentralization and dump on retail.
The Senate's approach is simple because it punts all the hard stuff to regulators.

items.rationale: Tokens deriving value from a controlled company (FTT-like) carry securities-grade risk profiles but get marketed as decentralized; threatens both consumers and the legal premise distinguishing tokens from equity.

citations.quote: Company-backed tokens derive their value from systems that can't function absent human control.
FTT was a security.

items.rationale: Unsolved disclosure, Gary-Plastic, and below-NAV problems — manageable for BTC/ETH/SOL but dangerous when teams of less-mature projects spin up and control their own public vehicles.

citations.quote: I'm not sure these problems are solvable where management teams of less mature projects are spinning up and controlling these public vehicles themselves.

items.rationale: Despite long-term enthusiasm, current systems are 'riddled with bugs,' protected more by obscurity than proven security, with >100,000x prover overhead; formal verification years away. Not ready for high-stakes deployments.
items.age_note: Performance optimism since 2024 paired with explicit 2025-03 security warning

citations.quote: while SNARKs and zkVMs show immense promise, they're not ready for complex, high-stakes deployments. Bugs are everywhere
they need to be secure they're not now they're riddled with bugs

items.rationale: Unfettered access to secondary liquidity (SPAC/ICO analog) lets insiders hype-launch-dump, producing vaporware and retail harm; disclosure alone is insufficient — favors lockups tied to decentralization.

citations.quote: Unfettered access to secondary market liquidity is an existential threat to innovation.

items.rationale: PBS decentralizes validation but centralizes block-building ('proof-of-MEV' big-get-bigger); exclusive order flow and multi-block/cross-chain MEV worsen it. Counsel against rushing to regulate MEV — let it evolve transparently onchain.

citations.quote: PBS is reducing centralization in validation, but might create centralization in building because of this disproof of MEV competition

items.rationale: Canonical 'network token' — value accrues via EIP-1559 burn (automatic buyback), expected to be deflationary; explicitly not a security. The model the house holds up as correct token design.

citations.quote: ETH is not a security, nor should transactions in ETH be securities transactions.
long term I think most of us expect eth to be a deflationary currency

items.rationale: Praised for pragmatic, shipping-focused engineering culture driving major growth; cited as home of liquid financial markets and recent stablecoin growth. Spot SOL ETF seen as near.

citations.quote: There are no zealots in Solana, only pragmatic engineers... shipping beats maximalism

items.rationale: Backed with conviction via an additional $70M token purchase with lockup; framed as a new paradigm for verifiable, credibly-neutral applications.

citations.quote: we've invested an additional $70M in @eigencloud tokens (EIGEN) with a lockup.

items.rationale: Backed with an additional $55M token purchase with 3-year lockup; protocol enabling new businesses and complex workflows to move onchain.

citations.quote: we've invested an additional $55M in @LayerZero_Core tokens (ZRO), with a 3-year lockup.

items.rationale: Repeatedly cited as a clean 'network token' whose network effects accrue to holders and which can capture protocol fees (fee switch / UniStaker) — contrasted favorably vs company-backed tokens.

citations.quote: UNI can capture fees from the network (the switch is off right now) via a mechanism like UniStaker.

items.rationale: Respected as store of value and the most secure/decentralized network ('not a memecoin'); strong institutional ETP flows. Note: team is lukewarm on a US strategic BTC reserve as a priority, not on the asset itself.

citations.quote: being the native asset of what is arguably the most secure and decentralized network gives it economic characteristics... unlike any memecoin.

items.rationale: The recurring cautionary archetype: a synthetic profits-interest in a centralized company that should have been regulated as a security; used to show why 'economic reality' (not legal form) must govern classification.
items.age_note: Used as the canonical negative example throughout 2024–2025

citations.quote: FTT is a great example. It does not matter that it didn't grant tokenholders any legal rights... it functioned as a profits interest in FTX

items.rationale: Ownership of nothing; pure speculation that conflates with productive tokens and damages industry perception. Categorically distinct from (and inferior to) network tokens.

citations.quote: Memecoins are the opposite of equity — ownership of nothing versus something.

items.rationale: Tokens where teams hold the bulk of supply and retain control retain securities-grade trust dependencies; cited as why market-structure rules restricting insiders are needed to prevent rug pulls.

citations.quote: So looks like a completely centralized network token... Not all network tokens remove the trust dependencies of ordinary securities.
Market structure legislation is needed now more than ever to prevent rug pulls and consumer harm.

risk_regime.rationale: By Aug 2025 the house is decisively constructive: the existential regulatory fight has subsided (SEC dropping cases, GENIUS Act signed, CLARITY advancing with broad support), infrastructure is finally app-ready (sub-cent/sub-second stablecoin transfers), stablecoin volumes and institutional ETP flows at all-time highs, and they repeatedly call this the best-ever time to build. This is a sharp REVERSAL from 2024's defensive crouch (lawfare, debanking, 'expect enforcement actions'). Optimism is tempered, not negated, by tactical concerns: getting market-structure legislation right, avoiding memecoin/speculation excess, treasury-company fragility, and zkVM immaturity — i.e., risk-on on fundamentals while warning against repeating get-rich-quick mistakes.

notes: Corpus is multi-author (the a16z crypto house: Dixon on networks/IP/social, Jennings on token-classification/regulation, Lazzarin & Matsuoka on tokens/metrics, Thaler on SNARKs/Jolt, Yahya on infra/DePIN/AI, Broner on stablecoins, Kominers on market design/NFTs). The dominant 2025 throughline is a regulatory-clarity-driven optimism replacing 2024's regulatory-siege framing — the single biggest recency-weighted shift; older 'crypto is under attack / expect enforcement' statements (e.g. 2024-11-06, 2024-12) are superseded. A persistent, cycle-independent doctrine underlies everything: decentralization = absence of CONTROL, network tokens must accrue real economic value, and speculation/memecoins are a tax on the industry's credibility. ZK is the clearest internally-split view (structurally bullish, tactically cautious on bugs/perf). Conviction scores are intensity of stated enthusiasm/concern, not probability of success. Token entries reflect both verbal endorsement and capital commitment (EIGEN, ZRO lockups) as conviction signals; FTT appears only as a negative archetype, not a live position.
